visa_types.name: Housemaid
visa_types.documents_required:
Housemaid Checklist (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2kAtmluyooUFr7AgBHE56X/8d372afc79587689a1ca18cd1268e6e9/Greece-Checklist-Housemaid.pdf)
A duly filled/signed “Sponsor’s Guarantee (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/311cNFczz7EYlkUcn9O8Nf/8ec55e9b5d409d517abd1ec3fcae9f6c/Sponsers.pdf)” is required for each housemaid applicant.
INFORMATION
For Persons applying for tourist Visa (stay not more than 90 days)
DOCUMENTS REQUIRED
- Original passport and copy of previous Schengen visas.
For new passports, the old passport is also required. Passport should be
valid for at least six (6) months
from the date of visa issuance.
- Two colored passport-size photos
of good quality (white background) must be in compliance with ICAO standards.
- Visa application completed in English and signed
(name should be written as in passport).
- Copy of Bahrain residence permit and civil ID.
Applicants should be
residents of Bahrain
and have a
valid residence of at least six (6) months
(for non-Bahraini and GCC citizens).
- Sponsor’s Guarantee Letter.
(For non-Bahraini nationals) Stating the purpose of travel, salary, and start date of employment with the same sponsor in Bahrain.
                                                                            (Please attach copy of sponsor’s passport
civil ID).
(Must  be working with sponsor for at least 3 years).
- Sponsor’s Bank statement
for the last six (6) months with present balance (Note: If printed through the internet, bank stamp is required).
- Copy of employment contract.
- International medical travel insurance
for the duration of the trip covered not less than 30.000 Euros.
- Ticket and voucher of hotel reservation
visa_types.visa_fees:
Please Note: There is a service charge of BHD 8.200 per application (inclusive of 10% VAT) over and above the visa fees payable by all applicants

[grc > bhr > en > Table:Housemaid > Visa Fees 1]
The Visa fees & service charge can be paid in BHD by cash only. It is subject to change without notice.
                                                                        
All fees are non-refundable
** The Greece Ministry has given list of certain nationalities that pay a Reduced visa fee of BHD 19. Please check the website / at Visa Application Centre for it.
Reduced visa fee (19 BHD) is charged for the following countries citizens: 
- Albania (for holders of non-biometric passports only. Holders of biometric passports are exempted from visa requirement)
- Armenia
- Azerbaijan
- Bosnia and Herzegovina (for holders of non-biometric passports only. Holders of biometric passports are exempted from visa requirement)
- North Macedonia (for holders of non-biometric passports only. Holders of biometric passports are exempted from visa requirement)
- Georgia (for holders of non-biometric passports only. Holders of biometric passports are exempted from visa requirement)
- Moldova (for holders of non-biometric passports and/or passports not issued in line with standards of the International Civil Aviation Organisation (ICAO). Holders of biometric
                                                                                passports passports issued in line with the standards of the International Civil Aviation Organisation (ICAO) are exempted from visa).
- Montenegro (for holders of non-biometric passports only. Holders of biometric passports are exempted from visa requirement)
- Serbia (for holders of non-biometric passports only. Holders of biometric passports are exempted from visa requirement)
- Ukraine (for holders of non-biometric passports only. Holders of biometric passports are exempted from visa requirement)
No visa fee is charged for the following categories:
- Children below the age of 12 years.
- School pupils, students, postgraduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake stays for the purpose of study or educational training.
- Researchers from third countries travelling for the purpose of carrying out scientific research as defined in Recommendation No 2005/761/EC of the European Parliament and of the
                                                                                Council of 28 September 2005 to facilitate the issue by the Member States of uniform short-stay visas for researchers from third countries travelling within the Community
                                                                                for the purpose of carrying out scientific research.
- Young people aged 25 years or less participating in seminars, conferences, sports, cultural or educational events organised by non-profit organisations.
- Holders of diplomatic and service passports.
- EU/EEA family members (i.e.spouses, children under 21, dependent parents).
- Holders of Georgian Biometric Ordinary Passports. The visa waiver does not cover the non-Biometric Ordinary Passports.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Once received by the Visa Application Centre, applications will be sent to the Embassy of Greece in Kuwait the next working day & the applicant will be given an appointment for a personal
                                                                        Interview (if needed) with the Embassy of Greece in Kuwait. Once the assessment is completed by the Embassy, the decision together with the passport & the other relevant documents
                                                                        will be available for collection from the Visa Application Centre.
Visa is valid to visit all the Schengen States. Only Bahraini nationals and nationals of third countries that are legal residents in Bahrain can apply for a visa at the Greece Visa Application
                                                                        Centre in Bahrain. Visa applicants are requested to appear in person at the time of submission. 
Incomplete, unclear or unsigned applications will not be accepted.
Visa fees are non-refundable and have to be paid only in cash at the time of application
If the visit involves two or more Schengen countries, the Embassy responsible for the issuance of the visa will be: 
- The Embassy of the country in which the traveler is going to stay the longest.
- If the traveler is to stay the same time in different Schengen countries, the Embassy of the country of first entry.
Applicant’s lack of Travel plan will not fall on Embassy priority to  process your visa. It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid  any inconvenience
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Once received by the Visa Application Centre, applications will be sent to the Embassy of Greece in Kuwait the next working day & the applicant will be given an appointment for a personal
                                                                        Interview (if needed) with the Embassy of Greece in Kuwait. Once the assessment is completed by the Embassy, the decision together with the passport & the other relevant documents
                                                                        will be available for collection from the Visa Application Centre.
Visa is valid to visit all the Schengen States. Only Bahraini nationals and nationals of third countries that are legal residents in Bahrain can apply for a visa at the Greece Visa Application
                                                                        Centre in Bahrain. Visa applicants are requested to appear in person at the time of submission. 
Incomplete, unclear or unsigned applications will not be accepted.
Visa fees are non-refundable and have to be paid only in cash at the time of application
If the visit involves two or more Schengen countries, the Embassy responsible for the issuance of the visa will be: 
- The Embassy of the country in which the traveler is going to stay the longest.
- If the traveler is to stay the same time in different Schengen countries, the Embassy of the country of first entry.
Applicant’s lack of Travel plan will not fall on Embassy priority to  process your visa. It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid  any inconvenience

## Visa Fees
Please Note: There is a service charge of BHD 8.200 per application (inclusive of 10% VAT) over and above the visa fees payable by all applicants

[grc > bhr > en > Table:Housemaid > Visa Fees 1]

- Armenia

- Montenegro (for holders of non-biometric passports only. Holders of biometric passports are exempted from visa requirement)
- Serbia (for holders of non-biometric passports only. Holders of biometric passports are exempted from visa requirement)
- Ukraine (for holders of non-biometric passports only. Holders of biometric passports are exempted from visa requirement)
No visa fee is charged for the following categories:
- Children below the age of 12 years.
- School pupils, students, postgraduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake stays for the purpose of study or educational training.
- Researchers from third countries travelling for the purpose of carrying out scientific research as defined in Recommendation No 2005/761/EC of the European Parliament and of the

                                                                                for the purpose of carrying out scientific research.
- Young people aged 25 years or less participating in seminars, conferences, sports, cultural or educational events organised by non-profit organisations.
- Holders of diplomatic and service passports.
- EU/EEA family members (i.e.spouses, children under 21, dependent parents).
- Holders of Georgian Biometric Ordinary Passports. The visa waiver does not cover the non-Biometric Ordinary Passports.

DOCUMENTS REQUIRED
- Original passport and copy of previous Schengen visas.
For new passports, the old passport is also required. Passport should be
valid for at least six (6) months
from the date of visa issuance.
- Two colored passport-size photos

Applicants should be

                                                                            (Please attach copy of sponsor’s passport
civil ID).
(Must  be working with sponsor for at least 3 years).
- Sponsor’s Bank statement
for the last six (6) months with present balance (Note: If printed through the internet, bank stamp is required).
- Copy of employment contract.

- Ticket and voucher of hotel reservation

## Photo Specifications
Photograph Guidelines (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4aXl2m33HReRwRycqYHsXU/f88c4950137f352132b0a1327f8878fc/photo_spec.pdf)
Applicants are requested to submit their application form with the new photo format, as explained below: 
General
The photo must fulfill the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization).
                                                                    
- Use plain light-coloured background.
- The photo must be no more than 6-months old.
- Not tilted picture (portrait style) showing both edges of face clearly.
- Close up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80% of the photograph.
- Photo in sharp focus and clear.
Photo Size
The photo for each visa applicant must measure:
                                                                    
- The picture dimensions must be 240 pixels wide by 320 pixels high at least. A smaller picture will be rejected. For instance, a photo being 235 pixels by 320 pixels is not valid.
- On the other hand, the photo should keep a proportion of 3 x 4 (width x height) within limits. However, 250 x 340 pixels picture would be valid, but not a 290 x 340 pixels one.
- Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes.
Resolution
- An image resolution is measured in DPI (dots per inch) and it's mainly used to control printing quality. Therefore, higher resolution means better printing quality.
- Photos resolution should be 300 dpi, so, when you scan the image, check this parameter carefully. However, bear in mind that the higher the resolution, the bigger the picture.
Resolution
- The only image format accepted is JPEG (Join Photograph Expert Grup Format). The other image formats such as BMP, TIFF, GIF, PNG, etc. will be rejected (a warning will be shown).
Attaching the Photo to the Application form
- Glue one photograph to the application form in the designated space.
Note : If children are traveling with their parents and are included inside the passport of the mother and/or the father, one photograph for each children MUST be glued to the application form of the father or the mother, depending on whom are they traveling with.
Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.

## Processing Time
Please visit http://www.mfa.gr/missionsabroad/en/kuwait-en/visas (http://www.mfa.gr/missionsabroad/en/kuwait-en/visas)
- Applications shall be decided on within 15 calendar days of the date of the delivery to the Embassy of an application which is admissible and may be extended upto 30 calendar days or in exceptional cases upto 60 calendar days
- Applications shall be decided on within 10 calendar days of the date of the delivery to the Embassy of an application which is admissible for the citizens of the following countries
                                                                            due to Visa Facilitation Agreements:
- Armenia, Azerbaijan, FYROM, Georgia, Russian Federation, Ukraine.
- Albania, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Moldova, Montenegro, Serbia (For holders of non-biometric passports only, since holders of biometric passports are exempted from visa requirement).
- Applications of EU/EEA family members shall be decided as soon as possible and no later than 15 calendar days of the date of the delivery to the Embassy of an application which is
                                                                            admissible.
Note:  The applications are delivered to the Embassy in maximum three working days for VFS Bahrain.

## Download Forms
Please take a printout of the Visa application Form. You are required to fill in all details of the applicant in a legible handwriting and submit it along with the required documents
                                                                        to the Visa Application Center.
You can refer to the contact us page from the address and the location map. Also, refer to the documents required page for the required documents to be submitted along with the application
                                                                        form.
Application for Schengen Visa (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4ExDZF6RaLla8wyMypiyO9/684839c4a82f0e355a68cb71a695ce84/Updated_Application_Form-Greece.pdf)
Sponsor’s Guarantee (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/311cNFczz7EYlkUcn9O8Nf/8ec55e9b5d409d517abd1ec3fcae9f6c/Sponsers.pdf)

visa_types.name: Transit Visa
visa_types.documents_required:
Transit visa Checklist (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3NlT6KhnQfHeu98KIwC39F/b9ca5146cb6a59233f84f406019c9008/Greece-Checklist-Transit.pdf)
Airport visa allows you to wait for a connecting flight in the international area of the airport. This visa does not grant access to the Schengen area. Only some nationalities require
                                                                        an Airport Transit Visa Documents to be submitted  with the visa application:
- A visa application form correctly completed and signed
- Two (02) recent color photographs (3.5 cm × 4 cm) in compliance with ICAO standards.
- Original Passport and copy of previous Schengen visas. The passport should be valid for at least six (6) months from the travel date and contain at least two (02) free pages.
- In case of a new passport, photocopies of the old passport are required 
Copy of Bahrain Residence Permit (for Non-Bahraini) / Civil ID copy (for GCC citizens). Residence permit should be valid for at least 6 months. 
- International Travel Insurance
- A valid entry visa for the final country of destination
- A proof of transport arrangements (confirmed airplane ticket or bookings)
Important
- The above mentioned visa is for Airport Transit only and not valid for any other Schengen country
- It is compulsory for both the parents to visit the Visa Application Centre in order to sign the Minor’s Travel Permission form at the submission counter itself [for the kids travelling
                                                                            with or without parent(s)].
- APPLICATIONS SHOULD AS A RULE, BE SUBMITTED BY THE APPLICANT. IN SPECIAL CASES (DISTANCE etc.) THEY COULD BE SUBMITTED BY ANY OTHER PERSON HAVING A SPECIAL POWER OF ATTORNEY. THE
                                                                            CONSULATE MAY AT ANY TIME REQUEST THE PRESENCE OF THE APPLICANT FOR AN INTERVIEW AS WELL AS REQUEST ADDITIONAL DOCUMENTS

- It is compulsory for both the parents to visit the Visa Application Centre in order to sign the Minor’s Travel Permission form at the submission counter itself [for the kids travelling
                                                                            with or without parent(s)].
- APPLICATIONS SHOULD AS A RULE, BE SUBMITTED BY THE APPLICANT. IN SPECIAL CASES (DISTANCE etc.) THEY COULD BE SUBMITTED BY ANY OTHER PERSON HAVING A SPECIAL POWER OF ATTORNEY. THE
                                                                            CONSULATE MAY AT ANY TIME REQUEST THE PRESENCE OF THE APPLICANT FOR AN INTERVIEW AS WELL AS REQUEST ADDITIONAL DOCUMENTS

## Photo Specifications
Photograph Guidelines (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4aXl2m33HReRwRycqYHsXU/f88c4950137f352132b0a1327f8878fc/photo_spec.pdf)
Applicants are requested to submit their application form with the new photo format, as explained below: 
General
The photo must fulfill the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization).
                                                                    
- Use plain light-coloured background.
- The photo must be no more than 6-months old.
- Not tilted picture (portrait style) showing both edges of face clearly.
- Close up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80% of the photograph.
- Photo in sharp focus and clear.
Photo Size
The photo for each visa applicant must measure:
                                                                    
- The picture dimensions must be 240 pixels wide by 320 pixels high at least. A smaller picture will be rejected. For instance, a photo being 235 pixels by 320 pixels is not valid.
- On the other hand, the photo should keep a proportion of 3 x 4 (width x height) within limits. However, 250 x 340 pixels picture would be valid, but not a 290 x 340 pixels one.
- Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes.
Resolution
- An image resolution is measured in DPI (dots per inch) and it's mainly used to control printing quality. Therefore, higher resolution means better printing quality.
- Photos resolution should be 300 dpi, so, when you scan the image, check this parameter carefully. However, bear in mind that the higher the resolution, the bigger the picture.
Resolution
- The only image format accepted is JPEG (Join Photograph Expert Grup Format). The other image formats such as BMP, TIFF, GIF, PNG, etc. will be rejected (a warning will be shown).
Attaching the Photo to the Application form
- Glue one photograph to the application form in the designated space.
Note : If children are traveling with their parents and are included inside the passport of the mother and/or the father, one photograph for each children MUST be glued to the application form of the father or the mother, depending on whom are they traveling with.
Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.

## Processing Time
Please visit http://www.mfa.gr/missionsabroad/en/kuwait-en/visas (http://www.mfa.gr/missionsabroad/en/kuwait-en/visas)
- Applications shall be decided on within 15 calendar days of the date of the delivery to the Embassy of an application which is admissible and may be extended upto 30 calendar days or in exceptional cases upto 60 calendar days
- Applications shall be decided on within 10 calendar days of the date of the delivery to the Embassy of an application which is admissible for the citizens of the following countries
                                                                            due to Visa Facilitation Agreements:
- Armenia, Azerbaijan, FYROM, Georgia, Russian Federation, Ukraine.
- Albania, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Moldova, Montenegro, Serbia (For holders of non-biometric passports only, since holders of biometric passports are exempted from visa requirement).
- Applications of EU/EEA family members shall be decided as soon as possible and no later than 15 calendar days of the date of the delivery to the Embassy of an application which is
                                                                            admissible.
Note:  The applications are delivered to the Embassy in maximum three working days for VFS Bahrain.

                                                                        to the Visa Application Center.

visa_types.overview:
SCHENGEN VISAS:
Currently, the Embassy of Greece in Kuwait is competent for examining and deciding on applications for visiting Greece as main destination.
Foreign nationals who wish to travel to those countries (mentioned above) have to be in possession of a Schengen visa, unless they are nationals from countries which are not included in the list
                                                            of countries with the obligation to carry a visa in order to cross external borders for stays in the Schengen Area of no more than three months (90 days) in a period of six months (180 days)
                                                            from the date of entry into the Schengen Area. 
Only Bahraini nationals and nationals of third countries who are legal residents in Bahrain can apply for the Schengen visa at the Embassy of Greece, Kuwait and through the Visa Application Center.
Due to the increasing volume of applications, the number of appointments for applying for a Schengen visa at the Embassy's office has been restricted in order to provide a good service. For that
                                                            reason, kindly anticipate well in advance and apply AT LEAST 15 labor days before expected date of travel in order to avoid any future disappointment. Applications may also be submitted at the
                                                            VFS Global (as an outsourced partner by the Embassy of Greece in Kuwait) as per the instructions given in the web page.
The same can be also submitted at the Embassy of Greece in Kuwait only with prior appointment requested at least 1 month before the proposed date of travel (no applicants will be received at the
                                                            Embassy without advanced registration and no applications will be received after visa office hours!).
- Short  Stay (https://www.vfsglobal.com/denmark/bahrain/short-term-visa.html) (Type C) 
- Tourist Visa
- Business Visa
- Housemaid
- Transit Visa
- Gambia Nationals
- EEA/EU Spouse Visa

All fees are non-refundable
** The Greece Ministry has given list of certain nationalities that pay a Reduced visa fee of BHD 19. Please check the website / at Visa Application Centre for it.
Reduced visa fee (19 BHD) is charged for the following countries citizens: 
- Albania (for holders of non-biometric passports only. Holders of biometric passports are exempted from visa requirement)
- Armenia
- Azerbaijan
- Bosnia and Herzegovina (for holders of non-biometric passports only. Holders of biometric passports are exempted from visa requirement)
- North Macedonia (for holders of non-biometric passports only. Holders of biometric passports are exempted from visa requirement)
- Georgia (for holders of non-biometric passports only. Holders of biometric passports are exempted from visa requirement)
- Moldova (for holders of non-biometric passports and/or passports not issued in line with standards of the International Civil Aviation Organisation (ICAO). Holders of biometric
                                                                                passports passports issued in line with the standards of the International Civil Aviation Organisation (ICAO) are exempted from visa).
- Montenegro (for holders of non-biometric passports only. Holders of biometric passports are exempted from visa requirement)
- Serbia (for holders of non-biometric passports only. Holders of biometric passports are exempted from visa requirement)
- Ukraine (for holders of non-biometric passports only. Holders of biometric passports are exempted from visa requirement)
No visa fee is charged for the following categories:
- Children below the age of 12 years.
- School pupils, students, postgraduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake stays for the purpose of study or educational training.

                                                                                Council of 28 September 2005 to facilitate the issue by the Member States of uniform short-stay visas for researchers from third countries travelling within the Community
                                                                                for the purpose of carrying out scientific research.
- Young people aged 25 years or less participating in seminars, conferences, sports, cultural or educational events organised by non-profit organisations.

- EU/EEA family members (i.e.spouses, children under 21, dependent parents).
- Holders of Georgian Biometric Ordinary Passports. The visa waiver does not cover the Non-Biometric Ordinary Passports.

- The Embassy of the country in which the traveler is going to stay the longest.
- If the traveler is to stay the same time in different Schengen countries, the Embassy of the country of first entry.
Applicant’s lack of Travel plan will not fall on Embassy priority to  process your visa. It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid  any inconvenience

For Persons applying for EEA/EU Family Visa (stay not more than 90 days)
DOCUMENTS REQUIRED
- Original passport and copy of previous Schengen visas. For new passports, the old passport is also required.

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Once received by the Visa Application Centre, applications will be sent to the Embassy of Greece in Kuwait the next working day & the applicant will be given an appointment for a personal
                                                                        Interview (if needed) with the Embassy of Greece in Kuwait. Once the assessment is completed by the Embassy, the decision together with the passport & the other relevant documents
                                                                        will be available for collection from the Visa Application Centre.
Visa is valid to visit all the Schengen States. Only Bahraini nationals and nationals of third countries that are legal residents in Bahrain can apply for a visa at the Greece Visa Application
                                                                        Centre in Bahrain. Visa applicants are requested to appear in person at the time of submission. 
Incomplete, unclear or unsigned applications will not be accepted.
Visa fees are non-refundable and have to be paid only in cash at the time of application
If the visit involves two or more Schengen countries, the Embassy responsible for the issuance of the visa will be: 
- The Embassy of the country in which the traveler is going to stay the longest.
- If the traveler is to stay the same time in different Schengen countries, the Embassy of the country of first entry.
Applicant’s lack of Travel plan will not fall on Embassy priority to  process your visa. It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid  any inconvenience

## Visa Fees
Please Note: There is a service charge of BHD 8.200 per application (inclusive of 10% VAT) over and above the visa fees payable by all applicants

[grc > bhr > en > Table:Eea/eu Spouse Visa > Visa Fees 1]
The service charge can be paid in BHD by cash only. It is subject to change without notice.
                                                                        
All fees are non-refundable
No visa fee is charged for the following categories
- EU/EEA family members (i.e. spouses, children under 21, dependent parents).

## Documents Required

EEA/EU Family Checklist (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/6GmqinN7eDWY5nmzTEWm1W/ac204ceec773b67323d230f84142fb2b/EEA-EU-Checklist-Latest.pdf)
For applicants below 18 years of age, a duly filled/signed “Minor’s Travel Permission” is required.
Please Note that both the Parents' Passport copies (even signature page), Civil ID copy and Residence Permit Copies (for non-Bahraini) to be attached with the application. If the parent(s)
                                                                        would be travelling along with kid(s), the valid visa copy should be attached (if any).
It is compulsory for both the parents to visit the Visa Application Centre in order to sign the Minor’s Travel Permission form at the submission counter itself [for the kids travelling
                                                                        with or without parent(s)]
INFORMATION
For Persons applying for EEA/EU Family Visa (stay not more than 90 days)
DOCUMENTS REQUIRED
- Original passport and copy of previous Schengen visas. For new passports, the old passport is also required.
- Two colored passport-size photos of good quality (white background)in compliance with ICAO standards.
- Visa application completed in English and signed (name should be written as in passport).
- Passport should be valid for at least six (6) months from the date of visa issuance.
- Copy of Bahrain Residence Permit (for Non-Bahraini) / civil ID copy (for GCC citizens. Bahrain residency should be valid for at least six (6) months (for non-Bahraini and
                                                                            GCC citizens).
- International medical travel insurance for the duration of the trip covered not less than 30,000 (thirty thousand) Euros.
- Copy of return Airline / Flight Ticket and confirmed voucher of hotel reservation
Note: If travelling to other Schengen areas/countries, flight and hotel vouchers for all destinations
                                                                            must be submitted. Applicant may be asked to submit the proof of payment.
- Original and 1 copy of the marriage certificate. The certificates provided must be issued from Greece (in case of Greek citizen’s family members) OR from the relevant EEA/EU
                                                                            member state (in case of EEA/EU citizen’s family member). In the last case, if they are not in English, they must be translated in English with a copy of the original document
                                                                            attached.
- Original or certified copy of passport of the EU/EAA citizen.
- Support letter from the EU/EAA citizen

## Photo Specifications

Photograph Guidelines (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4aXl2m33HReRwRycqYHsXU/f88c4950137f352132b0a1327f8878fc/photo_spec.pdf)
Applicants are requested to submit their application form with the new photo format, as explained below: 
General
The photo must fulfill the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization).
                                                                    
- Use plain light-coloured background.
- The photo must be no more than 6-months old.
- Not tilted picture (portrait style) showing both edges of face clearly.
- Close up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80% of the photograph.
- Photo in sharp focus and clear.
Photo Size
The photo for each visa applicant must measure:
                                                                    
- The picture dimensions must be 240 pixels wide by 320 pixels high at least. A smaller picture will be rejected. For instance, a photo being 235 pixels by 320 pixels is not valid.
- On the other hand, the photo should keep a proportion of 3 x 4 (width x height) within limits. However, 250 x 340 pixels picture would be valid, but not a 290 x 340 pixels one.
- Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes.
Resolution
- An image resolution is measured in DPI (dots per inch) and it's mainly used to control printing quality. Therefore, higher resolution means better printing quality.
- Photos resolution should be 300 dpi, so, when you scan the image, check this parameter carefully. However, bear in mind that the higher the resolution, the bigger the picture.
Resolution
- The only image format accepted is JPEG (Join Photograph Expert Group Format). The other image formats such as BMP, TIFF, GIF, PNG, etc. will be rejected (a warning will be shown).
Attaching the Photo to the Application form
- Glue one photograph to the application form in the designated space.
Note : If children are traveling with their parents and are included inside the passport of the mother and/or the father, one photograph for each children MUST be glued to the application form of the father or the mother, depending on whom are they traveling with.
Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.

## Processing Time
Please visit http://www.mfa.gr/missionsabroad/en/kuwait-en/visas (http://www.mfa.gr/missionsabroad/en/kuwait-en/visas)
- Applications of EU/EEA family members shall be decided as soon as possible and no later than 15 calendar days of the date of the delivery to the Embassy of an application which is
                                                                            admissible.
Note:  The applications are delivered to the Embassy in maximum three working days for VFS Bahrain.

## Download Forms
Please take a printout of the Visa application Form. You are required to fill in all details of the applicant in a legible handwriting and submit it along with the required documents
                                                                        to the Visa Application Center.
You can refer to the contact us page from the address and the location map. Also, refer to the documents required page for the required documents to be submitted along with the application
                                                                        form.
Application for Schengen Visa (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4ExDZF6RaLla8wyMypiyO9/684839c4a82f0e355a68cb71a695ce84/Updated_Application_Form-Greece.pdf)

[grc > bhr > en > Table:Tourist Visa > Visa Fees 1]
The Visa fees & service charge can be paid in BHD by cash only. It is subject to change without notice.
                                                                        
All fees are non-refundable
** The Greece Ministry has given list of certain nationalities that pay a Reduced visa fee of BHD 19. Please check the website / at Visa Application Centre for it.
Reduced visa fee (19 BHD) is charged for the following countries citizens: 
- Albania (for holders of non-biometric passports only. Holders of biometric passports are exempted from visa requirement)
- Armenia
- Azerbaijan
- Bosnia and Herzegovina (for holders of non-biometric passports only. Holders of biometric passports are exempted from visa requirement)
- North Macedonia (for holders of non-biometric passports only. Holders of biometric passports are exempted from visa requirement)
- Georgia (for holders of non-biometric passports only. Holders of biometric passports are exempted from visa requirement)
- Moldova (for holders of non-biometric passports and/or passports not issued in line with standards of the International Civil Aviation Organisation (ICAO). Holders of biometric
                                                                                passports passports issued in line with the standards of the International Civil Aviation Organisation (ICAO) are exempted from visa).
- Montenegro (for holders of non-biometric passports only. Holders of biometric passports are exempted from visa requirement)
- Serbia (for holders of non-biometric passports only. Holders of biometric passports are exempted from visa requirement)
- Ukraine (for holders of non-biometric passports only. Holders of biometric passports are exempted from visa requirement)
No visa fee is charged for the following categories:
- Children below the age of 12 years.
- School pupils, students, postgraduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake stays for the purpose of study or educational training.
- Researchers from third countries travelling for the purpose of carrying out scientific research as defined in Recommendation No 2005/761/EC of the European Parliament and of the
                                                                                Council of 28 September 2005 to facilitate the issue by the Member States of uniform short-stay visas for researchers from third countries travelling within the Community
                                                                                for the purpose of carrying out scientific research.
- Young people aged 25 years or less participating in seminars, conferences, sports, cultural or educational events organised by non-profit organisations.
- Holders of diplomatic and service passports.
- EU/EEA family members (i.e.spouses, children under 21, dependent parents).
- Holders of Georgian Biometric Ordinary Passports. The visa waiver does not cover the Non-Biometric Ordinary Passports.
visa_types.processing_time:
Please visit http://www.mfa.gr/missionsabroad/en/kuwait-en/visas (http://www.mfa.gr/missionsabroad/en/kuwait-en/visas)
- Applications shall be decided on within 15 calendar days of the date of the delivery to the Embassy of an application which is admissible and may be extended upto 30 calendar days or in exceptional cases upto 60 calendar days
- Applications shall be decided on within 10 calendar days of the date of the delivery to the Embassy of an application which is admissible for the citizens of the following countries
                                                                            due to Visa Facilitation Agreements:
- Armenia, Azerbaijan, FYROM, Georgia, Russian Federation, Ukraine.
- Albania, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Moldova, Montenegro, Serbia (For holders of non-biometric passports only, since holders of biometric passports are exempted from visa requirement).
- Applications of EU/EEA family members shall be decided as soon as possible and no later than 15 calendar days of the date of the delivery to the Embassy of an application which is
                                                                            admissible.
Note:  The applications are delivered to the Embassy in maximum three working days for VFS Bahrain.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Once received by the Visa Application Centre, applications will be sent to the Embassy of Greece in Kuwait the next working day & the applicant will be given an appointment for a personal
                                                                        Interview (if needed) with the Embassy of Greece in Kuwait. Once the assessment is completed by the Embassy, the decision together with the passport & the other relevant documents
                                                                        will be available for collection from the Visa Application Centre.
Visa is valid to visit all the Schengen States. Only Bahraini nationals and nationals of third countries that are legal residents in Bahrain can apply for a visa at the Greece Visa Application
                                                                        Centre in Bahrain. Visa applicants are requested to appear in person at the time of submission. 
Incomplete, unclear or unsigned applications will not be accepted.
Visa fees are non-refundable and have to be paid only in cash at the time of application
If the visit involves two or more Schengen countries, the Embassy responsible for the issuance of the visa will be: 
- The Embassy of the country in which the traveler is going to stay the longest.
- If the traveler is to stay the same time in different Schengen countries, the Embassy of the country of first entry.
Applicant’s lack of Travel plan will not fall on Embassy priority to  process your visa. It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid  any inconvenience
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Once received by the Visa Application Centre, applications will be sent to the Embassy of Greece in Kuwait the next working day & the applicant will be given an appointment for a personal
                                                                        Interview (if needed) with the Embassy of Greece in Kuwait. Once the assessment is completed by the Embassy, the decision together with the passport & the other relevant documents
                                                                        will be available for collection from the Visa Application Centre.
Visa is valid to visit all the Schengen States. Only Bahraini nationals and nationals of third countries that are legal residents in Bahrain can apply for a visa at the Greece Visa Application
                                                                        Centre in Bahrain. Visa applicants are requested to appear in person at the time of submission. 
Incomplete, unclear or unsigned applications will not be accepted.
Visa fees are non-refundable and have to be paid only in cash at the time of application
If the visit involves two or more Schengen countries, the Embassy responsible for the issuance of the visa will be: 
- The Embassy of the country in which the traveler is going to stay the longest.
- If the traveler is to stay the same time in different Schengen countries, the Embassy of the country of first entry.
Applicant’s lack of Travel plan will not fall on Embassy priority to  process your visa. It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid  any inconvenience

## Visa Fees
Please Note: There is a service charge of BHD 8.200 per application (inclusive of 10% VAT) over and above the visa fees payable by all applicants

[grc > bhr > en > Table:Tourist Visa > Visa Fees 1]
The Visa fees & service charge can be paid in BHD by cash only. It is subject to change without notice.
                                                                        
All fees are non-refundable
** The Greece Ministry has given list of certain nationalities that pay a Reduced visa fee of BHD 19. Please check the website / at Visa Application Centre for it.
Reduced visa fee (19 BHD) is charged for the following countries citizens: 
- Albania (for holders of non-biometric passports only. Holders of biometric passports are exempted from visa requirement)
- Armenia

- North Macedonia (for holders of non-biometric passports only. Holders of biometric passports are exempted from visa requirement)
- Georgia (for holders of non-biometric passports only. Holders of biometric passports are exempted from visa requirement)
- Moldova (for holders of non-biometric passports and/or passports not issued in line with standards of the International Civil Aviation Organisation (ICAO). Holders of biometric
                                                                                passports passports issued in line with the standards of the International Civil Aviation Organisation (ICAO) are exempted from visa).
- Montenegro (for holders of non-biometric passports only. Holders of biometric passports are exempted from visa requirement)
- Serbia (for holders of non-biometric passports only. Holders of biometric passports are exempted from visa requirement)
- Ukraine (for holders of non-biometric passports only. Holders of biometric passports are exempted from visa requirement)
No visa fee is charged for the following categories:
- Children below the age of 12 years.
- School pupils, students, postgraduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake stays for the purpose of study or educational training.
- Researchers from third countries travelling for the purpose of carrying out scientific research as defined in Recommendation No 2005/761/EC of the European Parliament and of the

                                                                                for the purpose of carrying out scientific research.

- EU/EEA family members (i.e.spouses, children under 21, dependent parents).
- Holders of Georgian Biometric Ordinary Passports. The visa waiver does not cover the Non-Biometric Ordinary Passports.

## Documents Required
Tourist visa Checklist (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/tIyKIj4I0afeIkzftaLAh/f916114ed6145d39ca666aca7afa5a1e/Greece-Checklist-Tourist.pdf)
EU/EEA Family Member Checklist (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2B8gJxsQQlGEriYp8EgRSP/989b85fd0d09e74ab780a27433014f9f/EEA-EU-Family-Checklist.pdf)
Medical checklist (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2a1RaUKLTjvLmJiu5AtIVk/a8f2b11b1e701de8640bb3c04c6a6a9c/Checklist-Medical-KUWAIT-info.pdf)
Sports trip (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/1JTrHjhyfNjsyuqCWpk27b/910b4a9a571833d94e1d490ef136df2b/Sports-Team-Docs-Required-info.pdf)
For applicants below 18 years of age, a duly filled/signed “Minor’s Travel Permission (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/5uwQBykdqMaHwZTk5wjM4X/c9e29b40f2cbd1aa9cbf4f0383dd9284/Minors_travel.pdf)” is required.
                                                                        
Please Note that both the Parents' Passport copies (even signature page), Civil ID copy and Residence Permit Copies (for non-Bahraini) to be attached with the application.
                                                                        If the parent(s) would be travelling along with kid(s), the valid visa copy should be attached (if any).
It is compulsory for both the parents to visit the Visa Application Centre in order to sign the Minor’s Travel Permission form at the submission counter itself [for the kids travelling
                                                                        with or without parent(s)].
INFORMATION
For Persons applying for tourist Visa (stay not more than 90 days)
DOCUMENTS REQUIRED
- Original
passport and copy of previous Schengen  visas.
For new passports, the old passport is also required.
- Two colored passport-size photos
of good quality (white background)in compliance with ICAO standards.
- Visa application completed in English and signed
(name should be written as in passport).
- Passport should be
valid for at least six (6) months
from the date of visa issuance.
- Copy of Bahrain Residence Permit (for Non-Bahraini) / civil ID copy (for GCC citizens.
Bahrain  residency should be valid for at least six (6) months
(for non-Bahraini
                                                                            and GCC citizens).
- Salary certificate
and authorized signature of his which registered in Chamber of Commerce (Please attach copy of “Eatemad Tawkiea”).
- Bank statement
for the last six (6) months with present balance. (If acquired through the internet, issuing bank stamp is required).
- International medical travel insurance
for the duration of the trip covered not less than 30,000 (thirty thousand) Euros.
- Ticket and voucher of hotel reservation
Note: If travelling to other Schengen areas/countries, flight and hotel vouchers for all destinations must be submitted.
                                                                            Applicant may be asked to submit the proof of payment.
- For persons escorting a medical patient: confirmed hotel booking (full address of the hotel must be written in the application: full name, street name, building name & number, city
                                                                            and city code)

## Photo Specifications
Photograph Guidelines (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4aXl2m33HReRwRycqYHsXU/f88c4950137f352132b0a1327f8878fc/photo_spec.pdf)
Applicants are requested to submit their application form with the new photo format, as explained below: 
General
The photo must fulfill the norm specified by the ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization).
                                                                    
- Use plain light-coloured background.
- The photo must be no more than 6-months old.
- Not tilted picture (portrait style) showing both edges of face clearly.
- Close up of the head and top of the shoulders so that the face takes up 70–80% of the photograph.
- Photo in sharp focus and clear.
Photo Size
The photo for each visa applicant must measure:
                                                                    
- The picture dimensions must be 240 pixels wide by 320 pixels high at least. A smaller picture will be rejected. For instance, a photo being 235 pixels by 320 pixels is not valid.
- On the other hand, the photo should keep a proportion of 3 x 4 (width x height) within limits. However, 250 x 340 pixels picture would be valid, but not a 290 x 340 pixels one.
- Finally, the image weight (size in Kbytes) can never be bigger than 120 Kbytes.
Resolution
- An image resolution is measured in DPI (dots per inch) and it's mainly used to control printing quality. Therefore, higher resolution means better printing quality.
- Photos resolution should be 300 dpi, so, when you scan the image, check this parameter carefully. However, bear in mind that the higher the resolution, the bigger the picture.
Resolution
- The only image format accepted is JPEG (Join Photograph Expert Grup Format). The other image formats such as BMP, TIFF, GIF, PNG, etc. will be rejected (a warning will be shown).
Attaching the Photo to the Application form
- Glue one photograph to the application form in the designated space.
Note : If children are traveling with their parents and are included inside the passport of the mother and/or the father, one photograph for each children MUST be glued to the application form of the father or the mother, depending on whom are they traveling with.
Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photograph presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.

## Processing Time
Please visit http://www.mfa.gr/missionsabroad/en/kuwait-en/visas (http://www.mfa.gr/missionsabroad/en/kuwait-en/visas)
- Applications shall be decided on within 15 calendar days of the date of the delivery to the Embassy of an application which is admissible and may be extended upto 30 calendar days or in exceptional cases upto 60 calendar days
- Applications shall be decided on within 10 calendar days of the date of the delivery to the Embassy of an application which is admissible for the citizens of the following countries
                                                                            due to Visa Facilitation Agreements:
- Armenia, Azerbaijan, FYROM, Georgia, Russian Federation, Ukraine.
- Albania, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Moldova, Montenegro, Serbia (For holders of non-biometric passports only, since holders of biometric passports are exempted from visa requirement).
- Applications of EU/EEA family members shall be decided as soon as possible and no later than 15 calendar days of the date of the delivery to the Embassy of an application which is
                                                                            admissible.
Note:  The applications are delivered to the Embassy in maximum three working days for VFS Bahrain.

## Download Forms
Please take a printout of the Visa application Form. You are required to fill in all details of the applicant in a legible handwriting and submit it along with the required documents
                                                                        to the Visa Application Center.
You can refer to the contact us page from the address and the location map. Also, refer to the documents required page for the required documents to be submitted along with the application
                                                                        form.
